pattern: Replaying the coin-change DP from the smallest amount upward reveals each missing denomination exactly where the running way-count falls short.
hints: Process amounts from 1 upward, keeping the number of ways achievable with the denominations discovered so far.
If the recorded ways for an amount exceed what your current coins explain, that amount itself must be a denomination; if they fall short, no set works.

Start with one way to make zero, and for each amount compare the DP value to the target: a shortfall means add that amount as a coin and fold it into the DP, an overshoot means return empty, and a match means move on.

edge_inputs.description: no valid set explains the counts
first amount unreachable forbids the unit coin
single amount, no coin of value one
single amount with the unit coin
all zeros means the empty coin set

stress.description: build numWays from a random coin subset so solvable cases dominate, with small n for the brute oracle
stress.note: small n keeps the combinatorial oracle fast; some perturbed (unsolvable) cases included
